description: Convert between Markdown and HTML.

parameters.name: sourceField
parameters.description: The field containing the content to convert.

parameters.name: destinationKey
parameters.description: The field name for the converted output.

description: Compare two input datasets to find matching, different, and unique items.

parameters.name: mergeByFields
parameters.description: Define which fields to use for matching items between the two inputs.

nestedParameters.name: field1
nestedParameters.description: Field name from Input A to match on.

nestedParameters.name: field2
nestedParameters.description: Field name from Input B to match on.

parameters.name: resolve
parameters.description: How to handle items that match but have different values in other fields.

options.name: Use Input A Version
options.description: Output the Input A version of differing items

options.name: Use Input B Version
options.description: Output the Input B version of differing items

options.name: Use a Mix of Versions
options.description: Select which fields to prefer from each input

options.name: Include Both Versions
options.description: Include fields from both inputs, suffixed with _A and _B for conflicts

parameters.name: preferWhenMix
parameters.description: Which input to prefer for most fields.

parameters.name: exceptWhenMix
parameters.displayName: For These Fields, Prefer the Other Input
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to take from the non-preferred input.

nestedParameters.name: fuzzyCompare
nestedParameters.description: Tolerate type differences when comparing (e.g., number 3 equals string "3").

nestedParameters.name: fieldsToSkipComparing
nestedParameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to exclude from the same/different comparison.

nestedParameters.name: disableDotNotation
nestedParameters.description: When true, treats field names as literal strings (no nested access).

nestedParameters.name: multipleMatches
nestedParameters.description: How to handle when multiple items match.

options.name: Include First Match Only
options.description: Only include the first match found

description: Remove items matching a condition. Items that pass go to 'Kept', others go to 'Discarded'.

parameters.name: conditions
parameters.description: The conditions to evaluate for each item.

nestedParameters.name: value1
nestedParameters.description: The value or field reference to test (left side). Supports dot notation.

nestedParameters.name: dataType
nestedParameters.description: The data type of the value being compared.

nestedParameters.name: operation
nestedParameters.description: The comparison operation to perform.

nestedParameters.name: value2
nestedParameters.description: The second value to compare against (right side). Not needed for exists, empty, isTrue, isFalse operations.

parameters.name: combineOperation
parameters.description: How to combine multiple conditions.

options.name: OR
options.description: At least one condition must be true

parameters.name: ignoreCase
parameters.description: Whether to ignore letter case when evaluating string conditions.

parameters.name: looseTypeValidation
parameters.description: Try to convert values to the expected type before comparing (e.g. string '123' to number 123).

description: Triggers the workflow on a time-based schedule using either a fixed interval or a cron expression.

parameters.name: rule
parameters.description: How the schedule should be defined.

options.name: Interval
options.description: Trigger at a fixed time interval

options.name: Cron Expression
options.description: Trigger based on a cron expression

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: The interval in seconds between each trigger.

parameters.name: cronExpression
parameters.description: The cron expression defining the schedule (e.g., '0 0 * * *' for daily at midnight).

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: The timezone for the schedule (e.g., 'America/New_York').

description: Remove duplicate items from the input based on field comparison.

parameters.name: compare
parameters.description: How to identify duplicate items.

options.name: All Fields
options.description: Compare all fields of each item

options.name: All Fields Except
options.description: Compare all fields except the specified ones

parameters.name: fieldsToExclude
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of field names to exclude from comparison.

parameters.name: fieldsToCompare
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of field names to use for comparison.

nestedParameters.name: disableDotNotation
nestedParameters.description: When enabled, field names with dots are treated literally instead of as nested paths.

nestedParameters.name: removeOtherFields
nestedParameters.description: When enabled, only the comparison fields are kept in the output.

description: Starts when called by the Execute Sub-Workflow node. Define expected input fields or accept all data from the parent workflow.

parameters.name: notice
parameters.description: When an Execute Sub-Workflow node calls this workflow, the execution starts here. Any data passed into that node will be output by this node.

parameters.name: inputSource
parameters.description: How to handle incoming data from the parent workflow.

options.name: Accept All Data
options.description: Use all incoming data from the parent workflow unchanged

options.name: Define Using Fields Below
options.description: Define expected input fields with names and types

options.name: Define Using JSON Example
options.description: Infer input schema from an example JSON object

parameters.name: workflowInputs
parameters.description: Define expected input fields. Only these fields will be passed through from the parent workflow. Missing fields will be set to null.

nestedParameters.name: name
nestedParameters.description: A unique name for this workflow input, used to reference it from the parent workflow

nestedParameters.name: type
nestedParameters.description: Expected data type for this input value

parameters.name: jsonExampleNotice
parameters.description: Provide an example JSON object to infer field names. Only the top-level keys will be used as the input schema. Set a value to null to allow any type for that field.

parameters.name: jsonExample
parameters.description: An example JSON object. Top-level keys become the expected input fields.

description: Route items to different outputs based on conditions. Items matching the conditions go to the 'true' output, others go to 'false'.

parameters.name: conditions
parameters.description: The conditions to evaluate for each item.

nestedParameters.name: value1
nestedParameters.description: The first value to compare. Supports dot notation (e.g., 'json.user.name').

nestedParameters.name: operation
nestedParameters.description: The comparison operation to perform.

nestedParameters.name: value2
nestedParameters.description: The second value to compare against (not needed for isEmpty, isNotEmpty, isTrue, isFalse).

parameters.name: combineOperation
parameters.description: How to combine multiple conditions.

options.name: OR
options.description: At least one condition must be true

description: Passes data through without any changes. Useful as a placeholder or pass-through node.
Aggregate items with operations like count, sum, average, min, max. Optionally group by fields.

parameters.name: fieldsToSummarize
parameters.description: Fields and operations to apply.

nestedParameters.name: field
nestedParameters.description: The field to aggregate.

nestedParameters.name: aggregation
nestedParameters.description: The aggregation operation to perform.

parameters.name: splitBy
parameters.description: Comma-separated field names to group results by (like GROUP BY in SQL).

nestedParameters.name: disableDotNotation
nestedParameters.description: When enabled, field names with dots are treated literally.

nestedParameters.name: continueIfFieldNotFound
nestedParameters.description: Continue processing if a specified field does not exist in an item.

nestedParameters.name: separator
nestedParameters.description: Separator to use when concatenating values.

description: Split an array field in each item into separate output items.

parameters.name: fieldToSplit
parameters.description: The name of the array field to split. Supports dot notation (e.g., 'json.items').

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Which other fields from the original item to include alongside each split element.

options.name: All Other Fields
options.description: Include all other fields from the original item

options.name: Selected Fields
options.description: Include only specified fields from the original item

parameters.name: fieldsToInclude
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of field names to include from the original item.

description: Route items to different outputs based on rules or expressions. Supports up to 4 output branches.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: How to determine which output to route items to.

options.name: Expression
options.description: Route based on an expression that returns the output index (0-3)

parameters.name: rules
parameters.description: Define rules to route items to different outputs.

nestedParameters.name: output
nestedParameters.description: The output index to route to (0-3).

nestedParameters.name: value1
nestedParameters.description: The first value to compare. Supports dot notation for field references.

nestedParameters.name: operation
nestedParameters.description: The comparison operation.

nestedParameters.name: value2
nestedParameters.description: The second value to compare against.

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description: An expression that returns the output index (0-3). Evaluated as a field reference on the item.

parameters.name: fallbackOutput
parameters.description: The output to use when no rules match (0-3).

description: Modify, add, or replace fields on items. Use manual mode to set individual fields or raw mode to replace with JSON.
subtitle: ={{$parameter.mode}}

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: How to set the fields.

options.name: Manual Mapping
options.description: Manually define fields with name, value, and type

options.name: Raw JSON
options.description: Replace item data with raw JSON

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: The fields to set on each item.

nestedParameters.name: name
nestedParameters.description: The name of the field to set. Supports dot notation for nested fields (e.g., 'user.email').

nestedParameters.name: value
nestedParameters.description: The value to set for the field.

nestedParameters.name: type
nestedParameters.description: The type to cast the value to.

parameters.name: rawJson
parameters.description: The raw JSON to replace item data with.

parameters.name: includeOtherFields
parameters.description: Whether to keep existing fields that are not explicitly set.

description: Limit the number of items to a specified maximum.

parameters.name: maxItems
parameters.description: If there are more items than this number, some are removed.

parameters.name: keep
parameters.description: When removing items, whether to keep the ones at the start or the end.

options.name: First Items
options.description: Keep items from the beginning of the list

options.name: Last Items
options.description: Keep items from the end of the list

description: Starts the workflow when an HTTP request is received at the configured path.

parameters.name: httpMethod
parameters.description: The HTTP method to listen for.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The webhook path to listen on. Use ':' for dynamic segments (e.g., 'webhook/:id').

parameters.name: authentication
parameters.description: The authentication method for incoming requests.

parameters.name: responseMode
parameters.description: When to respond to the webhook request.

options.name: Immediately
options.description: Respond as soon as the webhook is received

options.name: When Last Node Finishes
options.description: Respond after the entire workflow has finished executing

options.name: Using 'Respond to Webhook' Node
options.description: Use a separate Respond to Webhook node to control the response

parameters.name: webhookNotice
parameters.description: Insert a 'Respond to Webhook' node to control when and how you respond. More info

parameters.name: responseData
parameters.description: What data to include in the webhook response.

options.name: All Entries
options.description: Return all output entries as an array

options.name: First Entry JSON
options.description: Return the first entry as JSON

options.name: First Entry Binary
options.description: Return the first entry's binary data

parameters.name: responseBinaryPropertyName
parameters.description: The name of the binary property to return in the response.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Additional options for the webhook.

nestedParameters.name: binaryPropertyName
nestedParameters.description: The name of the field containing binary data in the request.

nestedParameters.name: ignoreBots
nestedParameters.description: Ignore requests from known bots and crawlers.

nestedParameters.name: ipWhitelist
nestedParameters.description: Comma-separated list of allowed IPs or CIDR ranges. Leave empty to allow all.

nestedParameters.name: noResponseBody
nestedParameters.description: Do not send a body in the response.

nestedParameters.name: rawBody
nestedParameters.description: Return the raw request body instead of parsed data.

nestedParameters.name: responseCode
nestedParameters.description: The HTTP status code to return.

nestedParameters.name: responseContentType
nestedParameters.description: Override the Content-Type header of the response.

nestedParameters.name: responseData
nestedParameters.description: Custom data to include in the immediate response body.

nestedParameters.name: responseHeaders
nestedParameters.description: Custom headers to include in the webhook response.

nestedParameters.name: responsePropertyName
nestedParameters.description: The name of the property from the first entry to return.

description: Combine separate items or field values together into individual items.

parameters.name: aggregate
parameters.description: How to aggregate the items.

options.name: Individual Fields
options.description: For each specified field, collect all values across items into an array

options.name: All Item Data
options.description: Collect all input items into a single array

parameters.name: fieldsToAggregate
parameters.description: Fields to aggregate. Each field's values across all items will become an array.

nestedParameters.name: fieldToAggregate
nestedParameters.description: The field to collect values from.

nestedParameters.name: renameField
nestedParameters.description: Optional: rename the field in the output. Leave empty to keep the original name.

parameters.name: destinationFieldName
parameters.description: The field name for the aggregated data array.

nestedParameters.name: disableDotNotation
nestedParameters.description: When enabled, field names with dots are treated literally.

nestedParameters.name: keepOnlyUnique
nestedParameters.description: Remove duplicate values from aggregated arrays.

description: Returns data for the webhook. Use with Webhook node when 'Respond' is set to 'Using Respond to Webhook Node'.

parameters.name: respondWith
parameters.description: The data that should be returned and send back as a response to the webhook.

options.name: All Incoming Items
options.description: Respond with all incoming items as a JSON array

options.name: First Incoming Item
options.description: Respond with the first incoming item's JSON data

options.name: JSON
options.description: Respond with a custom JSON body

options.name: Text
options.description: Respond with a custom text message

parameters.name: responseBody
parameters.description: The text to send as the response body.

parameters.name: responseJson
parameters.description: The JSON data to send as the response body.

parameters.name: redirectURL
parameters.description: The URL to redirect the webhook caller to.

parameters.name: responseDataSource
parameters.description: How to determine which binary data to respond with.

options.name: Automatically Detect
options.description: Use the first binary property found on the input item

options.name: Choose Field
options.description: Specify the binary field name to use

parameters.name: inputFieldName
parameters.description: The name of the binary field to use for the response.

nestedParameters.name: responseCode
nestedParameters.description: The HTTP status code for the response (100-599).

nestedParameters.name: responseHeaders
nestedParameters.description: Custom headers to include in the response.

nestedParameters.name: responseKey
nestedParameters.description: The key name to wrap the response data in.

description: Rename field names (keys) in the input items.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: The key renaming rules to apply.

nestedParameters.name: currentKey
nestedParameters.description: The current name of the key to rename.

nestedParameters.name: newKey
nestedParameters.description: The new name for the key.

nestedParameters.name: regexReplacement
nestedParameters.description: Use a regular expression to match and rename keys.

nestedParameters.name: searchRegex
nestedParameters.description: The regex pattern to match key names against.

nestedParameters.name: replaceRegex
nestedParameters.description: The replacement string (supports $1, $2, etc. for capture groups).

nestedParameters.name: maxDepth
nestedParameters.description: Maximum depth for nested renaming. -1 for unlimited, 0 for top-level only.

description: Run custom JavaScript or Python code to transform data.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: The programming language to use.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: How to run the code.

options.name: Run Once for All Items
options.description: Execute the code once with all input items available

options.name: Run Once for Each Item
options.description: Execute the code once for each input item

parameters.name: jsCode
parameters.description: The JavaScript code to execute.

parameters.name: pythonCode
parameters.description: The Python code to execute.

description: Manipulate dates and times: add, subtract, format, extract parts, calculate differences.

options.name: Get Current Date
options.description: Get the current date and time

options.name: Add to a Date
options.description: Add a duration to a date

options.name: Subtract from a Date
options.description: Subtract a duration from a date

options.name: Format a Date
options.description: Format a date into a different string format

options.name: Extract Date Part
options.description: Extract a specific part from a date (year, month, day, etc.)

options.name: Get Time Between Dates
options.description: Calculate the time difference between two dates

options.name: Round a Date
options.description: Round a date to the nearest unit

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date to operate on. Use an expression like ={{ $json.dateField }}.

parameters.name: date1
parameters.description: The start date for comparison.

parameters.name: date2
parameters.description: The end date for comparison.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: The number of units to add or subtract.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The output date format (Java DateTimeFormatter pattern, e.g. yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss).

parameters.name: outputFieldName
parameters.description: The field name for the result.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Timezone to use (e.g. UTC, America/New_York, Europe/London).

description: Combine data from two input branches. Supports append, key-based merge, and branch selection.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: How to combine the data from the two inputs.

options.name: Append
options.description: Append all items from Input 2 after Input 1

options.name: Combine by Matching Key
options.description: Merge items from both inputs by a matching key field

options.name: Choose Branch
options.description: Select all items from one input and discard the other

parameters.name: chooseBranchValue
parameters.description: Which input branch's data to output.

parameters.name: mergeKey
parameters.description: The field name to use for matching items between the two inputs (supports dot notation, e.g., 'json.id').

description: Generate HTML templates, extract content from HTML, or convert data to HTML tables.

options.name: Generate HTML Template
options.description: Generate HTML from a template using item data

options.name: Extract HTML Content
options.description: Extract content from HTML using CSS-like tag selectors

options.name: Convert to HTML Table
options.description: Convert input items to an HTML table

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: HTML template. Use {{ fieldName }} placeholders to inject data.

parameters.name: sourceField
parameters.description: The field containing HTML to extract from.

parameters.name: extractionValues
parameters.description: Define what to extract from the HTML.

nestedParameters.name: key
nestedParameters.description: The output key name for this extraction.

nestedParameters.name: cssSelector
nestedParameters.description: HTML tag to search for (e.g. 'h1', 'a', 'p', 'div.class').

nestedParameters.name: returnValue
nestedParameters.description: What to return from the matched element.

nestedParameters.name: attribute
nestedParameters.description: The attribute name to extract (e.g. 'href', 'src').

parameters.name: destinationKey
parameters.description: The field name for the output.

nestedParameters.name: tableStyle
nestedParameters.description: CSS style for the table element.

nestedParameters.name: headerStyle
nestedParameters.description: CSS style for th elements.
nestedParameters.defaultValue: border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 8px; text-align: left; background-color: #f2f2f2;

nestedParameters.name: cellStyle
nestedParameters.description: CSS style for td elements.

description: Run custom JavaScript code. The code receives 'items' and should return the modified items array.

parameters.name: functionCode
parameters.description: The JavaScript code to execute. Use 'items' to access input data. Must return an array of items.

description: Sort items by field values, randomly, or using custom code.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: How to sort the items.

parameters.name: sortFieldsUi
parameters.description: Add fields to sort by. Fields are evaluated in order — earlier fields take priority.

nestedParameters.name: fieldName
nestedParameters.description: The field to sort by. Supports dot notation for nested fields (e.g., 'user.name').

nestedParameters.name: order
nestedParameters.description: The sort direction.

nestedParameters.name: disableDotNotation
nestedParameters.description: When enabled, field names with dots are treated literally instead of as nested paths.

description: Process items in batches. Connect the 'Loop' output through processing nodes back to this node's input to iterate.

parameters.name: batchSize
parameters.description: The number of items to return with each iteration.

nestedParameters.name: reset
nestedParameters.description: Start again from the beginning of the input items, even if items are still remaining from a previous run.

description: Convert between JSON and XML formats.

options.name: XML to JSON
options.description: Parse XML string into JSON data

options.name: JSON to XML
options.description: Convert JSON data into an XML string

parameters.name: dataField
parameters.description: The field containing the XML string (for XML to JSON) or JSON data (for JSON to XML).

parameters.name: destinationKey
parameters.description: The field name for the converted output.

nestedParameters.name: rootElement
nestedParameters.description: The root XML element name (for JSON to XML).

nestedParameters.name: headless
nestedParameters.description: Omit the XML declaration (<?xml ...?>).

nestedParameters.name: prettyPrint
nestedParameters.description: Format the XML output with indentation.

description: Makes an HTTP request and returns the response data.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The HTTP method to use.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to make the request to.

parameters.name: authentication
parameters.description: The authentication method to use.

options.name: Generic Credential Type
options.description: Use generic authentication (header, query, or basic)

parameters.name: nodeCredentialType
parameters.description: Select which type of credential to use.

options.name: Venafi TLS Protect Cloud API
options.description: Venafi TLS Protect Cloud API authentication

options.name: Microsoft Graph Security OAuth2 API
options.description: Microsoft Graph Security OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Google Business Profile OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Business Profile OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: HTTP Basic Auth
options.description: Basic authentication with username and password

options.name: Google Cloud Natural Language OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Cloud Natural Language OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Google OAuth2 API
options.description: Google OAuth2 authentication for Google services

options.name: Microsoft To Do OAuth2 API
options.description: Microsoft To Do OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: LinkedIn Community Management OAuth2 API
options.description: LinkedIn Community Management OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Azure Storage Shared Key API
options.description: Azure Storage Shared Key API authentication

options.name: Microsoft OAuth2 API
options.description: Microsoft OAuth2 authentication for Microsoft services

options.name: Google Cloud Storage OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Cloud Storage OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Google Firebase Cloud Firestore OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Firebase Cloud Firestore OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Venafi TLS Protect Datacenter API
options.description: Venafi TLS Protect Datacenter API authentication

options.name: Google Firebase Realtime Database OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Firebase Realtime Database OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Facebook Lead Ads OAuth2 API
options.description: Facebook Lead Ads OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Google Big Query OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Big Query OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Azure Cosmos DB Shared Key API
options.description: Azure Cosmos DB Shared Key API authentication

options.name: Google Workspace Admin OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Workspace Admin OAuth2 API authentication

options.name: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)
options.description: App-only (client credentials) authentication for APIs secured by Microsoft Entra ID

options.name: Google Sheets Trigger OAuth2 API
options.description: Google Sheets Trigger OAuth2 API authentication

parameters.name: genericAuthType
parameters.description: Select the generic authentication mechanism.

parameters.name: sendQuery
parameters.description: Whether to send query parameters.

parameters.name: queryParameters
parameters.description: The query parameters to send.

parameters.name: sendHeaders
parameters.description: Whether to send custom headers.

parameters.name: headerParameters
parameters.description: The custom headers to send.

parameters.name: sendBody
parameters.description: Whether to send a request body.

parameters.name: contentType
parameters.description: The content type of the request body.

options.name: Raw
options.description: Send raw text with custom content type

parameters.name: specifyBody
parameters.description: How to specify the JSON body.

parameters.name: jsonBody
parameters.description: The JSON body to send with the request.

parameters.name: bodyParameters
parameters.description: Key-value pairs for the request body.

parameters.name: formParameters
parameters.description: Key-value pairs for form body.

parameters.name: multipartParameters
parameters.description: Fields to send as multipart/form-data.

parameters.name: binaryPropertyName
parameters.description: The name of the input binary field containing the file data.

parameters.name: rawContentType
parameters.description: The MIME type for the raw body.

parameters.name: rawBody
parameters.description: The raw body content.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Additional options for the request.

nestedParameters.name: allowUnauthorizedCerts
nestedParameters.description: Allow connections to servers with self-signed or untrusted SSL certificates.

nestedParameters.name: arrayFormat
nestedParameters.description: How to format arrays in query parameters.

nestedParameters.name: lowercaseHeaders
nestedParameters.description: Convert all header names to lowercase before sending.

nestedParameters.name: proxy
nestedParameters.description: HTTP proxy URL (e.g. http://proxy:8080).

nestedParameters.name: followRedirects
nestedParameters.description: Whether to follow HTTP redirects.

nestedParameters.name: maxRedirects
nestedParameters.description: Maximum number of redirects to follow.

nestedParameters.name: timeout
nestedParameters.description: Request timeout in milliseconds. 0 means no timeout.

nestedParameters.name: responseFormat
nestedParameters.description: How to parse the response body.

options.name: Autodetect
options.description: Automatically detect JSON or return as text

nestedParameters.name: outputPropertyName
nestedParameters.description: The property name for binary or file output.

nestedParameters.name: fullResponse
nestedParameters.description: Return the full response including status code and headers.

nestedParameters.name: neverError
nestedParameters.description: Succeed even when the HTTP status code is not 2xx.

nestedParameters.name: pagination
nestedParameters.description: Automatically handle paginated API responses.

options.name: Response Contains Next URL
options.description: The response contains a URL for the next page

options.name: Response Contains Cursor
options.description: Use a cursor from the response

nestedParameters.name: paginationNextUrl
nestedParameters.description: Expression to extract the next page URL from the response (e.g. 'next' or 'links.next').

nestedParameters.name: paginationMaxRequests
nestedParameters.description: Maximum number of pages to fetch. 0 means unlimited.

nestedParameters.name: paginationPageParam
nestedParameters.description: The query parameter name for the page number.

nestedParameters.name: paginationCursorParam
nestedParameters.description: The query parameter name for the cursor.

nestedParameters.name: paginationCursorPath
nestedParameters.description: Dot-notation path to extract the cursor from the response (e.g. 'meta.next_cursor').

parameters.name: batching
parameters.description: Send requests in batches with a delay between each batch.

parameters.name: batchSize
parameters.description: Number of requests per batch.

parameters.name: batchInterval
parameters.description: Delay in milliseconds between batches.

parameters.name: provideSslCertificates
parameters.description: Whether to provide custom SSL certificates.

parameters.name: sslCertificateNotice
parameters.description: To use SSL certificates, you need to add them to the 'SSL Certificates' credential.

description: Starts the workflow on manual trigger. Use this as the entry point for workflows that are executed manually.

parameters.name: notice
parameters.description: This node is the starting point for workflows that are triggered manually. It will execute once when you click 'Execute Workflow'.

description: Execute another workflow and use its output data. The target workflow should use an Execute Workflow Trigger as its starting node.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow to execute. The target workflow should have an Execute Workflow Trigger as its starting node.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: How to pass data to the sub-workflow.

options.name: Run Once With All Items
options.description: Pass all input items to the sub-workflow in a single execution

options.name: Run Once For Each Item
options.description: Execute the sub-workflow separately for each input item and merge results

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Additional options.

nestedParameters.name: waitForCompletion
nestedParameters.description: Wait for the sub-workflow to complete and return its output. When disabled, the node completes immediately without output.

description: Perform cryptographic operations: hashing, HMAC, and random generation.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The cryptographic operation to perform.

options.name: Hash
options.description: Generate a hash of the input value

options.name: HMAC
options.description: Generate an HMAC of the input value

parameters.name: hashType
parameters.description: The hash algorithm to use.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to hash. Use an expression like ={{ $json.fieldName }} to hash a specific field.

parameters.name: secret
parameters.description: The secret key for HMAC.

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: The output encoding for the hash.

parameters.name: propertyName
parameters.description: The field name to store the result in.

parameters.name: generateType
parameters.description: What to generate.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: The length of the generated value (characters for password, bytes for hex).
